title: Microsoft Foundry LLMOps Demo — Retail Personalised Agent
narrative: A retail organization wants an intelligent assistant that can personalize retail recommendations across Australian brands such as Kmart, Officeworks, and Bunnings. The LLMOps engineer needs to manage prompts, create and version a Foundry agent, personalize the experience, compare models, evaluate quality, review monitoring, apply governance, and automate the loop with GitHub Actions.

stages.title: Intro / Scenario
stages.objective: Set the scene: retail personalization challenge and why LLMOps matters.
key_points: Retail orgs need intelligent assistants that personalize across brands.
LLMOps brings engineering discipline to the AI lifecycle.
Microsoft Foundry provides managed agent hosting, versioning, and governance.

stages.title: Prompt Management
stages.objective: Show that prompts are versioned assets, not ad-hoc strings.
key_points: Multiple prompt variants exist for different goals (cost, quality, grounding).
Prompt changes affect behavior and must be tracked.
Prompt management is the first step in the LLMOps loop.

stages.title: Create / Update Agent in Foundry
stages.objective: Demonstrate managed agent creation and versioning in Microsoft Foundry.
key_points: Agents are managed resources in Foundry with versioning.
Creating a new version is non-destructive.
The agent is tied to a model deployment and instructions.

stages.title: Baseline Run
stages.objective: Run the agent with default prompt and no personalization.
key_points: Establishes a baseline for comparison.
Uses gpt-4.1-mini as the primary model.
Shows raw agent behavior before optimization.

stages.title: Persona Personalization
stages.objective: Inject customer persona context and observe behavior change.
key_points: Persona data acts as lightweight memory/context.
LLMOps includes data + prompt + agent orchestration.
Different personas produce meaningfully different responses.

stages.title: Multi-Model Comparison
stages.objective: Compare model outputs to optimize quality, cost, and latency.
key_points: Model selection is part of the optimization loop.
Compare quality, style, latency, and consistency.
Results inform which model to promote.

stages.title: Evaluation
stages.objective: Run structured evaluations to measure quality.
key_points: Every change must be tested against fixed scenarios.
Scoring dimensions: relevance, personalization, grounding, policy/safety.
Evaluation makes the LLMOps loop measurable.

stages.title: Monitoring and Traces
stages.objective: Show what observability looks like in production.
key_points: Trace every invocation: model, prompt, persona, latency, result.
Foundry provides built-in tracing and monitoring.
Local demo captures runtime events for review.

stages.title: Governance and Guardrails
stages.objective: Demonstrate controls that keep AI safe and compliant.
key_points: Approved prompts, agent versioning, evaluation gates.
RBAC and least-privilege credentials.
Safety expectations and policy compliance.

stages.title: GitHub Actions / Automation
stages.objective: Show how CI/CD automates the LLMOps loop.
key_points: Prompt changes trigger CI validation.
Evaluation runs automatically on push.
Promotion is quality-gated.
